developer-org.comment: Built by Codeium; Codeium/Windsurf was acquired by Cognition AI in July 2025 and the product was later renamed Devin Desktop (June 2026). Checked 2026-07-09.

implementation-language.comment: Closed, bundled engine — the backend indexing/retrieval stack is not publicly disclosed. The Windsurf editor is a VS Code fork, but the indexing engine's implementation language is undocumented.

first-release.comment: Windsurf Editor (with its bundled indexing/context engine) launched November 2024. Codeium's underlying context-awareness engine predates this, but the bundled Windsurf indexing dates to the editor launch.

status.comment: Actively developed and sold; product renamed Devin Desktop in 2026 but remains active. Checked 2026-07-09.

open-source.comment: Bundled proprietary engine; source not published.

repository.comment: Closed-source bundled engine — no public source repository.

github-stars.comment: Closed-source bundled engine — no public repository to star.

form-factor.comment: The retrieval engine lives inside the Windsurf editor / Cascade agent; not shipped as a standalone MCP server, library, or CLI.

standalone-usable.comment: Welded into Windsurf/Cascade (and Codeium extensions) — cannot be bolted onto your own agent as an independent context provider.

bundled-host.comment: Ships inside the Windsurf editor (now Devin Desktop) and Codeium IDE extensions.

install-mechanism.comment: Obtained by downloading the Windsurf editor app (binary); the same context engine also surfaces via Codeium editor extensions for VS Code / JetBrains.

retrieval-technique.comment: RAG over an embeddings index, augmented by Windsurf's proprietary 'M-Query' LLM-based retrieval/ranking that the vendor says outperforms plain cosine-similarity search. Tagged embeddings; the M-Query LLM stage is captured under Reranking.

query-interface.comment: The Cascade agent issues natural-language / tool-call retrieval against the index; no user-facing regex query surface for the index itself.

returns.comment: Returns relevant code snippets/chunks retrieved for the agent's prompt context.

structural-awareness.comment: Vendor/secondary sources state the index is built over files, functions, classes, and type definitions, implying parser/structure awareness. Internals are not fully documented; based on stated behaviour.

chunking-strategy.comment: Reported to embed functions, classes, and type definitions as units. Exact chunking algorithm is undocumented; inferred from stated indexing granularity.

reranking.comment: 'M-Query' applies an LLM-based retrieval/ranking stage over first-pass results to improve precision beyond raw embedding similarity.

index-location.comment: Local indexing (index stored on the machine) by default; Teams/Enterprise can use a server-side Remote Indexing Service that indexes repos on Windsurf's isolated-tenant servers.

incremental-updates.comment: Only changed files are re-processed after the initial index build.

real-time-watch.comment: Index updates incrementally as files change during editing rather than requiring a manual re-index.

vector-store.comment: Backing store for the embeddings index is a proprietary, undisclosed component.

embedding-model.comment: Uses Codeium/Windsurf's own proprietary embedding model; the specific model is not publicly documented.

mcp-support.comment: Windsurf is an MCP client (it can call external MCP servers), but its own indexing engine is not exposed as an MCP server for other agents to consume.

agent-compatibility.comment: Usable only by its host (Windsurf/Cascade and Codeium extensions); not consumable by third-party agents.

ide-integrations.comment: Primary surface is the standalone Windsurf editor (a VS Code fork); the Codeium context engine also ships in VS Code and JetBrains extensions.

language-agnostic.comment: Embedding-based retrieval indexes arbitrary code/text; Codeium advertises support for 70+ languages, with best structural handling on well-supported languages.

supported-languages.value: 70+ languages (embeddings index arbitrary text; Codeium advertises 70+ supported languages)
supported-languages.comment: Codeium historically advertises 70+ programming languages; exact parsed-language set for indexing is not separately documented.

code-leaves-machine.comment: By default AI features send code/context to Windsurf's servers for inference. Enterprise Remote Indexing clones repositories to Windsurf's isolated-tenant servers to build embeddings, then deletes the code/snippets once embeddings are created (unless Store Snippets is enabled). No fully offline path for the AI retrieval.

local-only-option.comment: The local index sits on disk, but retrieval/generation requires Windsurf's hosted models/services — there is no fully offline, no-external-call mode.

self-hostable.comment: Enterprise offering includes on-premise / self-managed (hybrid) deployment options for organizations that must control where code is processed; not available on consumer plans.

telemetry.comment: Usage data is collected by default with settings/privacy options to opt out (and enterprise zero-data-retention options). Checked 2026-07-09.

max-repo-scale.comment: Handles large codebases (docs cite 50,000+ files); very large monorepos (100,000+ files) require configuring include/exclude scope. Enterprise remote indexing targets multi-repo scale.

pricing-model.comment: Indexing has no standalone price — it is bundled into Windsurf's plans (Free, Pro, Max, Teams, Enterprise).

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: No standalone price for the indexing engine; it is included in Windsurf plans (bundled). The Windsurf editor itself has a $0 Free tier and a $20/mo Pro tier as of the March 2026 pricing revamp, but that prices the whole product, not the indexer.

maturity.comment: Widely deployed, polished bundled engine inside a mature commercial IDE; not a full 5 given ongoing product churn (ownership change, rename to Devin Desktop) and undocumented, evolving internals.

active-maintenance.comment: Product actively developed — pricing/model updates through 2026 and continued integration under Cognition. Checked 2026-07-09.

community-size.comment: Large user base (Windsurf is a widely adopted AI IDE), though below Cursor-scale ('huge').
